message:
feat(cli): doctor sidecar cleanup, temp artifact tracking, migrate open path, robot sentinel support

Large set of CLI-side additions that teach the doctor command to
safely manage SQLite sidecars and scratch artifacts while running
recovery operations, plus support for the new "malformed
attachments" sentinel in robot output.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs

- `cleanup_stale_db_artifacts(db_path)` removes stale `-wal`,
  `-shm`, `.bak`, and doctor-scratch sidecars left over from a
  previous crashed or killed `doctor` run. Runs at the top of
  every doctor command that plans to open the primary database,
  so a half-deleted previous run can't poison the current one.

- `os_string_with_suffix(value, suffix)`,
  `path_with_file_name_suffix(path, suffix, fallback)`,
  `os_str_starts_with(value, prefix)`,
  `os_str_ends_with(value, suffix)` are the CLI-side equivalents
  of the same private helpers that pool.rs grew in the db crate.
  Having them local means the CLI does not pull the db internals
  into its module tree just to build sidecar path names.

- `open_db_sync_for_migrate(database_url)` opens the database
  specifically for the migration path: it bypasses the normal
  async pool setup, disables WAL auto-checkpointing, and attaches
  with a short busy_timeout so a wedged writer cannot block a
  recovery migration indefinitely.

- `remove_sqlite_sidecar_if_exists(sidecar_path, context)` removes
  a single sidecar file with a uniform error-context message so
  every doctor path that cleans sidecars logs the same shape of
  failure.

- `sqlite_artifact_conflicts(candidate)` returns true when a
  candidate path would collide with an existing SQLite artifact
  (primary, WAL, SHM, .bak). Used to decide whether a generated
  doctor-scratch path is safe to use or needs a fresh timestamp.

- `cleanup_replacement_sqlite_sidecars(...)` removes the sidecars
  for a replacement database before the swap step runs, so the
  swap does not promote the new database alongside the old
  database's WAL/SHM.

- `restore_swapped_replacement_sqlite_artifact_without_original(...)`
  handles the "original no longer exists" edge case in the swap
  path: if the swap step failed before the original was moved
  back, the replacement now needs to be promoted into the
  original's spot as-is instead of being rolled back onto a
  non-existent file.

- `cleanup_doctor_temp_sqlite_artifact(path)` is the "I failed,
  best-effort clean up my scratch file" call that every
  doctor path registers in its drop-guard so a panic or
  early-return does not leak a temp file into the storage root.

- `doctor_temp_sqlite_artifact_conflicts(candidate, extension)`
  is the temp-artifact variant of `sqlite_artifact_conflicts`
  that also checks for the documented extensions the doctor uses
  (`.reconstructed`, `.archive`, `.doctor-scratch`, etc.).

- `next_doctor_artifact_path_with_timestamp(...)` and
  `next_doctor_artifact_path(base_path, label, extension)` generate
  a unique doctor-scratch path with a UTC microsecond timestamp,
  looping on the conflict check so two concurrent doctor runs in
  the same microsecond produce distinct files.

- `seed_project_only_db(db_path, slug, human_key)` seeds a
  fresh project-only database used by the reconstruction path
  when the live db has no recoverable state at all. It is a
  deterministic fixture so the reconstructed db starts from a
  known-good shape.

- Numerous doctor subcommands now consult the mailbox health
  verdict (from the db crate) before choosing between the live db
  and the archive snapshot, mirroring the tools/server/mail_ui
  changes. The summary output reports which code path was taken
  for each project so the operator can see the verdict without
  having to re-run with debug logging.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/legacy.rs

Legacy CLI paths now route through the same sidecar helpers and
checkpoint delegation introduced in the core crate, so the legacy
backup path no longer duplicates checkpoint logic.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/robot.rs

- Robot JSON output recognizes the `malformed_attachments_json()`
  sentinel from the db crate and renders the canonical
  `{"malformed": true, "raw": "..."}` shape instead of emitting
  unparseable JSON to downstream robot consumers.
- Extended robot output for the doctor subcommands so the
  verdict-driven path choices are surfaced as structured fields
  in the JSON response, matching the TUI changes in
  server/mail_ui.

## Cargo.lock

Lock-file update pulling in the new transitive dependencies
required by the features above.
